target.name: aspera chaetominine B (revised structure)
target.motivation: Structural revision of aspera chaetominine B, which was initially misassigned. The natural product showed cytotoxic activity against leukaemia K562 and colon cancer cells SW1116, and significant protection against H1N1 virus-induced cytopathogenicity.

prior_syntheses.key_strategy: Isolation and structural elucidation, but with misassigned stereochemistry.

success_criteria.free_text: Synthesize the proposed structure of aspera chaetominine B (13) and compare its spectral data with the natural product. If different, synthesize a diastereomer and compare to revise the structure. The revised structure should match the known alkaloid (-)-isochaetominine C (6).

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-N bond forming the lactam ring
key_disconnections.reasoning: The final lactamization step is a key cyclization to form the hexacyclic core. An improved coupling system (EDCI/HOBt) was used to enhance efficiency.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C and C-N bonds forming the quinazolinone and indole rings
key_disconnections.reasoning: This cascade reaction is central to forming the complex polycyclic core from a tripeptide derivative, allowing for stereodivergent control based on work-up conditions.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The synthesis of aspera chaetominine B (13) and its revised structure (6) was approached using the group's established third-generation strategy for chaetominine-type alkaloids. This strategy involves a tripeptide derivative as a key intermediate, followed by a DMDO-oxidation triggered double cyclization and a final debenzylation-lactamization. The stereochemistry was controlled by the DMDO oxidation and subsequent work-up conditions. The initial synthesis of the proposed structure (13) was followed by a comparison of spectral data, which revealed a mismatch. This led to the synthesis of a diastereomer, which was then identified as the known (-)-isochaetominine C (6), thus revising the structure of aspera chaetominine B.

thought.background: Chaetominine-type alkaloids are a family of hexacyclic quinazolinone alkaloids with diverse stereochemistries and bioactivities. Several structures have been reported, and some have been revised through total synthesis.
thought.gap: The structures of aspera chaetominines A (12) and B (13) were recently reported, but their absolute configurations were not convincingly determined, and spectral data were recorded in different solvents, preventing direct comparison with known compounds.
thought.decision: To synthesize the proposed structures of aspera chaetominines A and B to confirm or revise their structures, leveraging the group's established stereodivergent synthetic strategies.

evidence.text_span: Several years after we have had accomplished the abovementioned investigations, Liu and co-workers reported the isolation and structural elucidation of two new alkaloids, aspera chaetominines A (12) and B (13) from marine sponge associated fungus Aspergillus versicolour SCSIO XWS04 F52 [32]. They reported that both the two alkaloids showed cytotoxic activity against leukaemia K562 and colon cancer cells SW1116 with IC50 ranged from 7.5 to 12.5 μM, and significant protection against H1N1 virus-induced cytopathogenicity in MDCK cells with IC50 values of 15.5 and 24.5 μM, respectively. Attracted by both their structure and bioactivities, we anticipated their synthesis. The author determined the structure by means of spectroscopic 1H, 13C NMR, HSQC, HMBC, and 1H·1H COSY), and MS analysis, and claimed that “their absolute configuration was unambiguously determined by the comparison with the reported compound chaetominine (1)” [32]. Such speculation regarding the absolute configurations is clearly not convincing. Moreover, neither optical rotation data nor melting point (both 12 and 13 were isolated as white powder) have been reported by Liu et al. [32]. Additionally, the solvents used for measuring 1H and 13C NMR are methanol-d4 that is different from Tan’s work [21] who used DMSO-d6 to record 1H and 13C NMR spectra of (–)-chaetominine (1). Thus we undertook the synthesis of the proposed structures of aspera chaetominines A (12) and B (13).
evidence.section: Synthesis of the proposed structures of aspera chaetominines A and B and revision of stereochemistry of aspera chaetominine B

thought.background: The group's first-generation strategy for chaetominine synthesis involves forming a tripeptide derivative from D-tryptophan and an L-amino acid methyl ester.
thought.gap: To synthesize the tripeptide derivative 25 for aspera chaetominine A (12).
thought.decision: To synthesize tripeptide derivative 25 from D-tryptophan (D-Trp) and L-isoleucine methyl ester hydrochloride salt (24) in three steps, following the first-generation strategy.

outcome.observation: Tripeptide derivative 25 was synthesized in three steps from D-Trp and L-Ile methyl ester hydrochloride salt (24).

evidence.text_span: By adopting our first-generation strategy [57,58,61], we re-synthesized tripepetide derivative 25 from D-tryptophan (D-Trp) and L-isoleucine (L-Ile) methyl ester hydrochloride salt (24) in three steps (Scheme 4).
evidence.section: Synthesis of the proposed structures of aspera chaetominines A and B and revision of stereochemistry of aspera chaetominine B

thought.background: The key step in the chaetominine synthesis is the DMDO oxidation-triggered double cyclization.
thought.gap: To convert tripeptide derivative 25 into the proposed structure of aspera chaetominine A (12) and the monocyclization product 26.
thought.decision: To treat 25 with DMDO in acetone, followed by work-up with a saturated aqueous solution of Na2SO3 at 30 °C.

outcome.observation: The proposed structure of aspera chaetominine A (12) was obtained in 31% yield, along with monocyclization product 26 in 45% yield. The spectral data of synthetic 12 were different from the reported natural product.

evidence.text_span: Treating 25 with DMDO followed by work-up with a saturated aqueous solution of Na2SO3 at 30 °C provided the proposed structure of aspera chaetominine A (12) and monocyclization product 26 in 31% and 45% yield, respectively. The spectral 1H and 13C NMR) data of our synthetic compound are different from those reported for the natural aspera chaetominine A, suggesting that the originally proposed stereochemistry for aspera chaetominine A (12) was incorrect.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the proposed structures of aspera chaetominines A and B and revision of stereochemistry of aspera chaetominine B

thought.background: The group's third-generation strategy for chaetominine synthesis involves using benzyl L-valinate as the coupling component.
thought.gap: To synthesize tripeptide derivative 28 for aspera chaetominine B (13).
thought.decision: To synthesize tripeptide derivative 28 in three steps using benzyl L-valinate.

outcome.observation: Tripeptide derivative 27 was synthesized in 89% yield. This was then converted to 28 in 97% yield.

evidence.text_span: Employing our third-generation strategy featuring the employment of benzyl L-valinate as the coupling component [63], tripeptide derivative 28 was synthesized in three steps.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the proposed structures of aspera chaetominines A and B and revision of stereochemistry of aspera chaetominine B

thought.background: The key step in the chaetominine synthesis is the DMDO oxidation-triggered double cyclization.
thought.gap: To convert tripeptide derivative 28 into the proposed structure of aspera chaetominine B (13) and the monocyclization product 29.
thought.decision: To expose 28 to DMDO in acetone, followed by treating the resulting intermediates with Na2SO3.

outcome.observation: The proposed structure of aspera chaetominine B (13) was obtained in 22% yield, along with monocyclization product 29 in 30% yield. The spectral data of synthetic 13 were different from the reported natural product.

evidence.text_span: Exposure of 28 to DMDO in acetone followed by treating the resulting intermediates with Na2SO3 produced the proposed structure of aspera chaetominine B (13) and monocyclization product 29 in 22% and 30% yield, respectively (Scheme 5). Once again, a comparison of the 1H and 13C NMR data of our synthetic compound 13 with those of the natural aspera chaetominine B showed that two compounds are different, indicating a misassignment of the structure (13) for aspera chaetominine B.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the proposed structures of aspera chaetominines A and B and revision of stereochemistry of aspera chaetominine B

thought.background: The synthesis of the proposed structure of aspera chaetominine B (13) yielded a compound whose spectral data did not match the natural product, indicating a misassignment.
thought.gap: To identify the correct structure of aspera chaetominine B.
thought.decision: To convert the monocyclization product 29 into a known chaetominine-type alkaloid through one-pot catalytic debenzylation-lactamization and compare its spectral data with the natural product.

outcome.observation: One-pot catalytic debenzylation–lactamization of 29 afforded lactamization product (–)-isochaetominine C (6) in 92% yield. The 1H and 13C NMR data of compound 6 matched those of natural aspera chaetominine B, confirming the structural revision.

evidence.text_span: To our delight, one-pot catalytic debenzylation–lactamization of 29 afforded lactamization product (–)-isochaetominine C (6). The 1H and 13C NMR data of compound 6 matched those of natural aspera chaetominine B suggesting that aspera chaetominine B is (–)-isochaetominine C (6), whose absolute configuration is tentatively assigned as 2R,3R,11S,14R.
evidence.section: Synthesis of the proposed structures of aspera chaetominines A and B and revision of stereochemistry of aspera chaetominine B

Overall Yield.interpretation: Overall yield for the synthesis of the revised structure (6) from D-Trp and L-Val benzyl ester is not explicitly stated as a single value, but individual step yields are given.

Longest Linear Steps.interpretation: The synthesis of the revised structure of aspera chaetominine B (6) was achieved in five steps.

validation.comparison_to_prior: The synthetic (–)-isochaetominine C (6) matched the reported 1H and 13C NMR data of natural aspera chaetominine B, confirming the structural revision. The initially proposed structure (13) did not match.
validation.significance: The total synthesis allowed for the revision of the misassigned structure of natural product aspera chaetominine B to (–)-isochaetominine C (6). This highlights the importance of total synthesis in structural elucidation and confirmation of natural products, especially when initial characterization data are incomplete or ambiguous.

source.title: Further elaboration of the stereodivergent approach to chaetominine-type alkaloids: synthesis of the reported structures of aspera chaetominines A and B and revised structure of aspera chaetominine B.
source.authors: Lü JF, Wu JF, Ye JL, Huang PQ.
